question: The maximum depth for emergency ascent skills training during any open water dive is:
explanation: Per SSI General Training Standards, controlled emergency swimming ascents (CESA) and other emergency-ascent skills are practiced from a maximum depth of 9 m / 30 ft. The shallow start keeps the ascent slow enough to manage and minimises lung-overexpansion risk if a student fails to exhale.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Student-to-instructor ratios:

options.a: Should not be reduced for any reason
options.b: Cannot be increased for any reason
options.c: Are determined entirely by the instructor
options.d: Are defined by the specific program standard

explanation: Each SSI program standard defines the maximum student-to-instructor ratio (e.g. OW confined 8:1, OW open water 8:1 with options to extend with certified assistants, Try Scuba 4:1, etc.). Instructors may always REDUCE ratios at their discretion for safety, but never exceed the program-defined maximum.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Once a student completes their academic and in-water training requirements:

options.a: Their certification must be processed within 7 days
options.b: They will automatically receive a digital certification card
options.c: A printed card may be ordered from the training center or regional SSI Service Center

explanation: All three statements apply: the instructor/training centre must process the certification within 7 days, the student receives a digital card automatically in their MySSI account, and a physical printed card can be ordered as an optional extra through the training centre or SSI Service Center.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Instructors may conduct SSI training programs if they are:

options.a: As long as they are affiliated with an SSI Training Center
options.b: In active or under review status

explanation: Only Active-status SSI Professionals may conduct training. Inactive members cannot teach (membership/insurance lapsed); 'under review' status indicates a quality-assurance investigation that suspends teaching authority; affiliation with a Training Center alone is not enough — the personal Pro status must be Active.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: When can an SSI Divemaster being used as a certified assistant evaluate skills?

options.c: During open water training dives only

explanation: Never. A certified assistant — including a Divemaster acting in that role — assists with logistics, supervision and safety, but skill EVALUATION is reserved to the instructor of record. The DM can demonstrate, position students, watch for issues, and report observations, but the pass/fail call on a skill is always the instructor's.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Limits for specific program depths are found:

options.a: In the Open Water Diver Instructor Manual
options.c: In the program's specific standards/instructor manual

explanation: Each SSI program (OW, Deep, Wreck, Cavern, Trimix, etc.) has its own specific standards / instructor manual that defines its own depth limits — 18 m for OW, 30 m for Deep specialty Level 1, 40 m for Level 2, etc. The GTS sets common rules but program-specific depths live in the program's own document.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Procedures for issuing diver referrals and replacing lost cards are found in:
explanation: Administrative procedures that apply across all programs — referrals (cross-instructor or cross-agency), card replacement, name changes, certification corrections — live in the General Training Standards under 'Certification Procedures', not in any single program manual.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: A written emergency plan is required:

options.b: For any location where training dives are conducted
options.d: Only if the instructor is not familiar with the dive site

explanation: An EAP (Emergency Action Plan) must be written and posted/available for any location where SSI training dives occur — pool, quarry, ocean — regardless of how well the instructor knows the site. It includes nearest chamber, emergency contacts, evacuation route, communication procedures.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: Which of the following statements is true about the SSI definition of a swim-through:

options.b: They do not need clearly visible entry and exit points
options.d: They can be used by entry-level students

explanation: SSI's swim-through definition includes a maximum length of 10 meters. A swim-through is distinct from cavern/wreck penetration: short, both ends clearly visible, with no overhead-environment certification required. Options A, B, D are wrong — there's no 30 m minimum depth, both ends MUST be clearly visible, and entry-level students CAN traverse a properly defined swim-through.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: The Water Fitness Evaluation must be completed before entry-level students:
explanation: The Water Fitness Evaluation (swim distance, water tread, comfort) is the gate to OPEN water training — it is performed in confined water as part of the early program and must be passed before the student progresses to any open water training dive. It does not have to precede the first pool session.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards

question: As defined in General Training Standards > Diving Environments, the Indoor Diving program requires:

options.b: A minimum surface area of 100 square meters

explanation: The Indoor Diving environment is defined by all three criteria: a minimum depth of 5 m, a minimum surface area of 100 m², and the requirement that the venue be an aquarium or purpose-built facility (not a recreational swimming pool). All three must be true for a venue to qualify.
source: mySSI: Instructor Pretest — Part 7 General Training Standards
